paper_title: Ordination Analysis and Study Area in North Sikkim, Higher Himalaya

methods.description: This method involves the use of ordination techniques such as PCA, RDA, and HCA to analyze the variation and clustering of diatom genera data from different sample locations. The analysis helps in understanding the influence of environmental factors like altitude and δ13C on the distribution of diatom species.

steps.description: PCA shows that first five components could only account for 52% of total variation in the genera data from different sample locations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to determine variance explained by components
outputs: percentage of variance explained by components

steps.description: RDA shows that a variation of 13.2% can only be constrained by δ13C and altitude.
steps.searchable_summary: perform RDA to assess variation constrained by δ13C and altitude

steps.description: HCA was carried out to see how these samples at three different localities are clustered based on dissimilarities between the diatom genera using the observed characteristics.
steps.searchable_summary: perform HCA to cluster samples based on diatom genera dissimilarities

steps.description: In Figure 6 d, PCA and HCA results are shown together for comparison.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize PCA and HCA results for comparison

steps.description: All the analyses were carried out in R platform (R Core Team, 2014), and the CRAN packages used were vegan and ape.
steps.searchable_summary: use R and CRAN packages vegan and ape for analysis
